exam.name: CompTIA Security+
exam.category_description: Master the foundational principles of cybersecurity and develop the practical skills required to secure networks, devices, and data.
exam.hero_headline: Launch Your Cybersecurity Career with CompTIA Security+.
exam.hero_subheadline: Master core security concepts, threat mitigation, and network architecture to pass the SY0-701 exam with confidence.
exam.companion_problem: Cybersecurity threats evolve rapidly, and certification exams cover a massive breadth of topics that are difficult to synthesize into a structured study plan.
exam.companion_solution: Only Ever breaks down the official CompTIA Security+ syllabus into 15-minute, highly focused study sessions. Our content maps directly to the SY0-701 exam objectives to ensure maximum retention and test readiness.
exam.learner_profile: Aspiring cybersecurity professional or IT administrator looking to validate baseline security skills. Works with networks, operating systems, and security protocols. Needs clear, practical explanations of vulnerabilities, threats, and defensive measures.

sections.title: General Security Concepts
sections.description: Understand foundational security controls, concepts like the CIA triad, Zero Trust frameworks, physical security, and cryptographic solutions.

topics.title: Types of Security Controls
topics.description: Understand the various categories and control types used to secure enterprise environments.
learning_objectives: Compare and contrast technical, managerial, operational, and physical control categories.
Explain preventive, deterrent, detective, corrective, compensating, and directive control types.

topics.title: Fundamental Security Concepts: CIA and AAA
topics.description: Explore the foundational cybersecurity principles of CIA and AAA.
learning_objectives: Define Confidentiality, Integrity, and Availability (CIA).
Explain non-repudiation and the phases of Authentication, Authorization, and Accounting (AAA).

topics.title: Zero Trust Architecture and Gap Analysis
topics.description: Learn how gap analysis and Zero Trust frameworks protect modern networks.
learning_objectives: Conduct a basic gap analysis.
Describe Zero Trust architecture components including the control plane and data plane.

topics.title: Physical Security and Deception Technologies
topics.description: Discover the physical security measures and deception technologies used against attackers.
learning_objectives: Identify physical security controls such as bollards, vestibules, and sensors.
Explain the use of honeypots, honeynets, honeyfiles, and honeytokens.

topics.title: Change Management Processes
topics.description: Understand how change management processes mitigate risks to security.
learning_objectives: Explain business and technical processes impacting security operations.
Identify essential documentation and version control practices during changes.

topics.title: Public Key Infrastructure (PKI) and Certificates
topics.description: Dive into the components of Public Key Infrastructure and digital certificates.
learning_objectives: Understand public and private keys within a PKI.
Differentiate between various certificate types and authorities.

topics.title: Cryptographic Concepts and Tools
topics.description: Examine encryption techniques, hashing, and hardware cryptographic tools.
learning_objectives: Compare full-disk, partition, and file-level encryption.
Identify use cases for TPM, HSM, obfuscation, hashing, and salting.

sections.title: Threats, Vulnerabilities, and Mitigations
sections.description: Identify common threat actors and vectors, examine vulnerabilities across applications and infrastructure, and apply suitable mitigations.

topics.title: Threat Actors and Motivations
topics.description: Identify common threat actors and what drives their malicious activities.
learning_objectives: Compare threat actor profiles such as nation-states, hacktivists, and insider threats.
Analyze actor motivations including financial gain, espionage, and disruption.

topics.title: Common Threat Vectors and Attack Surfaces
topics.description: Explore the primary channels and surfaces exploited by attackers.
learning_objectives: Define message-based, image-based, and file-based threat vectors.
Identify risks from unsecure networks, default credentials, and supply chains.

topics.title: Social Engineering Attacks
topics.description: Examine human vectors and the tactics used in social engineering.
learning_objectives: Recognize phishing, smishing, and vishing attempts.
Understand business email compromise, pretexting, and brand impersonation.

topics.title: Application and OS Vulnerabilities
topics.description: Analyze common application-level and operating system vulnerabilities.
learning_objectives: Explain memory injection, buffer overflows, and race conditions.
Identify vulnerabilities specific to operating systems and web applications like SQLi and XSS.

topics.title: Infrastructure Vulnerabilities
topics.description: Discover vulnerabilities affecting hardware, virtualization, and cloud systems.
learning_objectives: Understand risks associated with legacy hardware and end-of-life firmware.
Identify VM escape, cloud-specific, and supply chain vulnerabilities.

topics.title: Indicators of Malware Attacks
topics.description: Learn to recognize the signs and behaviors of different malware types.
learning_objectives: Differentiate between ransomware, Trojans, worms, and spyware.
Identify indicators such as logic bombs, keyloggers, and rootkits.

topics.title: Network and Physical Attacks
topics.description: Investigate common attacks directed at physical infrastructure and network layers.
learning_objectives: Explain brute force and RFID cloning physical attacks.
Analyze DDoS, DNS attacks, and credential replay on network infrastructure.

topics.title: Cryptographic and Password Attacks
topics.description: Study the methods attackers use to bypass cryptography and crack passwords.
learning_objectives: Understand downgrade, collision, and birthday cryptographic attacks.
Identify password spraying and brute force techniques alongside common attack indicators.

topics.title: Enterprise Mitigation Techniques
topics.description: Deploy structural and configuration mitigations to secure an enterprise.
learning_objectives: Implement segmentation, access controls, and application allow lists.
Apply hardening techniques, patching, and endpoint protection.

sections.title: Security Architecture
sections.description: Understand security implications of various architecture models, apply security principles to enterprise infrastructure, and protect organizational data.

topics.title: Architecture Models: Cloud, Serverless, and IaC
topics.description: Explore the security implications of cloud, serverless, and code-based architecture.
learning_objectives: Analyze the cloud shared responsibility matrix and hybrid considerations.
Understand the security of infrastructure as code, serverless computing, and microservices.

topics.title: Architecture Models: On-premises, Virtualization, and IoT
topics.description: Examine traditional, virtual, and specialized operational technology environments.
learning_objectives: Compare on-premises setups with centralized and decentralized architectures.
Identify security considerations for virtualization, IoT, ICS/SCADA, and embedded systems.

topics.title: Enterprise Infrastructure Security Principles
topics.description: Apply core security principles when designing and placing network devices.
learning_objectives: Determine optimal device placement, security zones, and attack surface reduction.
Analyze failure modes like fail-open and fail-closed.

topics.title: Secure Communication and Access Controls
topics.description: Learn how to secure network traffic and remote access using VPNs and modern frameworks.
learning_objectives: Select effective firewall types including WAF, UTM, and NGFW.
Implement VPNs, IPsec, SD-WAN, and Secure Access Service Edge (SASE).

topics.title: Data Concepts and Strategies
topics.description: Identify various data types and understand where data sovereignty applies.
learning_objectives: Differentiate between regulated, intellectual property, and financial data types.
Understand data states (at rest, in transit, in use) and geographic considerations.

topics.title: Data Classifications and Security Methods
topics.description: Apply classifications and protective methods to sensitive information.
learning_objectives: Classify data as sensitive, confidential, public, restricted, or critical.
Implement data security methods such as encryption, hashing, masking, and tokenization.

topics.title: High Availability and Site Considerations
topics.description: Plan for high availability and diverse platforms to ensure resilient operations.
learning_objectives: Implement load balancing and clustering for high availability.
Evaluate hot, cold, and warm site considerations for continuity of operations.

topics.title: Backups, Recovery, and Power
topics.description: Establish reliable backup routines, testing, and power redundancy.
learning_objectives: Plan backup strategies including offsite storage, encryption, and snapshots.
Design testing exercises and ensure power continuity with UPS and generators.

sections.title: Security Operations
sections.description: Manage security operations including computing baselines, identity and access management, vulnerability management, alerting, monitoring, and incident response.

topics.title: Secure Baselines and Hardening Targets
topics.description: Establish secure configurations and harden various computing resources.
learning_objectives: Deploy and maintain secure baselines across an environment.
Identify hardening targets including workstations, servers, and cloud infrastructure.

topics.title: Wireless, Mobile, and Application Security
topics.description: Secure mobile deployments, wireless networks, and application environments.
learning_objectives: Configure WPA3 and RADIUS for wireless security.
Implement mobile device management (MDM) and secure coding practices like input validation.

topics.title: Asset Management Lifecycle
topics.description: Manage hardware, software, and data from acquisition to secure disposal.
learning_objectives: Track assets through inventory and enumeration processes.
Ensure proper sanitization, destruction, and data retention during decommissioning.

topics.title: Vulnerability Identification Methods
topics.description: Execute vulnerability scans, threat feeds, and penetration tests.
learning_objectives: Differentiate between static analysis, dynamic analysis, and package monitoring.
Leverage OSINT, dark web feeds, and bug bounty programs to identify threats.

topics.title: Vulnerability Analysis, Response, and Remediation
topics.description: Analyze identified vulnerabilities and apply appropriate remediations.
learning_objectives: Use the CVSS framework to prioritize vulnerabilities and rule out false positives.
Implement patches, compensating controls, or process exceptions.

topics.title: Security Alerting and Monitoring Computing Resources
topics.description: Monitor systems and applications using aggregated logs and active alerting.
learning_objectives: Identify computing resources to monitor including systems and infrastructure.
Perform log aggregation, scanning, and alert tuning to reduce noise.

topics.title: Alert Response and Security Tools
topics.description: Utilize industry-standard tools to monitor enterprise security events.
learning_objectives: Deploy Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) systems.
Use SCAP, antivirus, DLP, SNMP traps, and NetFlow for network visibility.

topics.title: Enhancing Security: Firewalls and Filters
topics.description: Modify network capabilities like firewalls and IPS to defend against attacks.
learning_objectives: Configure firewall rules, access lists, and screened subnets.
Analyze IDS/IPS signatures and deploy effective web filtering.

topics.title: OS Security and Endpoint Capabilities
topics.description: Strengthen operating systems, email flow, and endpoint detection.
learning_objectives: Secure operating systems using Group Policy and SELinux.
Implement email security protocols (DMARC, DKIM, SPF) and deploy EDR/XDR solutions.

topics.title: Identity and Access Management Concepts
topics.description: Provision user accounts and manage permissions using identity federations.
learning_objectives: Manage the lifecycle of user accounts and permission assignments.
Implement single sign-on (SSO) with LDAP, OAuth, and SAML.

topics.title: Authentication, Passwords, and Privileged Access
topics.description: Enforce multifactor authentication, password policies, and privileged access.
learning_objectives: Configure MFA using biometrics, tokens, and location factors.
Establish password best practices and use PAM tools like password vaulting.

topics.title: Automation and Orchestration Use Cases
topics.description: Automate security operations to improve efficiency and enforce baselines.
learning_objectives: Use scripting for user provisioning, ticket creation, and CI/CD testing.
Evaluate the benefits and complexities of automated security guard rails.

topics.title: Incident Response Activities
topics.description: Navigate the phases of incident response from preparation to lessons learned.
learning_objectives: Execute the incident response process including containment, eradication, and recovery.
Conduct tabletop exercises and simulations for IR training.

topics.title: Root Cause Analysis and Digital Forensics
topics.description: Perform threat hunting, root cause analysis, and digital forensic procedures.
learning_objectives: Preserve evidence through chain of custody, acquisition, and legal holds.
Conduct root cause analysis to understand security incidents.

topics.title: Investigation Data Sources
topics.description: Leverage diverse data sources to support cybersecurity investigations.
learning_objectives: Analyze firewall, endpoint, OS, and network logs.
Interpret packet captures and metadata to piece together attack timelines.

sections.title: Security Program Management and Oversight
sections.description: Implement effective security governance, manage third-party risk, enforce compliance, and execute audits and awareness programs.

topics.title: Elements of Effective Security Governance
topics.description: Summarize the guidelines, policies, and roles central to security governance.
learning_objectives: Draft effective Information Security and Acceptable Use policies.
Define roles and responsibilities for data owners, controllers, and custodians.

topics.title: The Risk Management Process: Identification and Assessment
topics.description: Conduct ad hoc and continuous risk assessments across the enterprise.
learning_objectives: Identify enterprise risks and execute regular risk assessments.
Utilize risk registers and establish key risk indicators.

topics.title: Risk Analysis, Tolerance, and Strategies
topics.description: Perform qualitative and quantitative risk analysis and determine treatment strategies.
learning_objectives: Calculate Single Loss Expectancy (SLE) and Annualized Loss Expectancy (ALE).
Apply risk management strategies including transfer, accept, avoid, and mitigate.

topics.title: Third-party Risk Assessment and Management
topics.description: Evaluate and manage the security risks introduced by vendors and supply chains.
learning_objectives: Conduct vendor assessments, penetration testing, and supply chain analysis.
Draft and review NDAs, SLAs, MOUs, and MSAs.

topics.title: Effective Security Compliance and Privacy
topics.description: Maintain regulatory compliance and uphold data privacy standards.
learning_objectives: Monitor compliance and understand the consequences of non-compliance.
Implement privacy controls recognizing legal implications and data subject rights.

topics.title: Types and Purposes of Audits and Assessments
topics.description: Differentiate between various internal and external audits and penetration tests.
learning_objectives: Prepare for internal self-assessments and external regulatory examinations.
Define penetration testing types including physical, offensive, defensive, and integrated.

topics.title: Implementing Security Awareness Practices
topics.description: Develop and execute effective security awareness training for end users.
learning_objectives: Conduct phishing campaigns and train users to recognize anomalous behaviors.
Provide guidance on insider threats, password management, and social engineering.
